name: Product Security and Telecommunications Infrastructure Act 2022
owner: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
description: UK statute (c. 46) regulating the security of consumer connectable products (Part 1) and telecommunications infrastructure deployment (Part 2). Part 1 sets baseline cybersecurity requirements for IoT devices placed on the UK market, with implementing regulations referencing ETSI EN 303 645. In force since 2024-04-29.
relationToCloudSecurity: Applies to consumer-connectable products that depend on cloud services. Part 1 security requirements include vulnerability-disclosure policies and minimum support periods.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to consumer-connectable products that incorporate AI capabilities.

license.notes: UK legislation published under the Open Government Licence v3.0 (compatible with CC BY 4.0). Attribution required.

lifecycle.notes: Royal Assent 2022-12-06; Part 1 security regime in force 2024-04-29 alongside The Product Security and Telecommunications Infrastructure (Security Requirements for Relevant Connectable Products) Regulations 2023 (SI 2023/1007).

current_extraction.notes: Authoritative source is legislation.gov.uk CLML (Crown Legislation Markup Language) XML at https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2022/46/data.xml (committed as psti.xml). Parsed via the shared extract_uk_legislation.py CLML parser into 80 per-section structured CSV/JSON entries plus 1 Schedule.
